description: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) secondary-electron images of Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steel alloy 9Al-10Mn-1C (wt.%) after tempering at 800 °C for 30 min, recorded at magnifications ranging from ×1,000 (10 μm scale bar) to ×10,000 (1 μm scale bar). The micrographs exhibit two distinct microstructural morphologies: fine-scale modulated or acicular precipitates within a matrix, and coarser aligned lamellar colonies consisting of lath-like carbides. The caption states that the former correspond to spinodal decomposition into austenite (γ) plus nano-sized κ-carbides, whereas the latter represent cellular transformation producing κ-carbides + ferrite (α) + residual austenite (γ) lamellar structures. The progressive zoom from low to high magnification illustrates the coexistence of nanoscale precipitated regions and micron-scale cellular lamellae, supporting the text conclusion that 9Al-10Mn-1C at 800 °C develops dual transformation microstructures responsible for outstanding hardness among the 9% Al alloys.
